content.canonical_text: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that enables AI applications to securely access tools and data from external sources. MCP uses a client-server architecture where AI applications (clients) connect to servers that provide resources, tools, and prompts. Capability discovery in MCP works through a negotiation mechanism: during initialization, the client sends its capabilities and the server responds with its supported capabilities. Tools are discovered via tools/list and called via tools/call. Resources use a URI-based addressing scheme with resource templates for parameterized access.
